title: Lesson 3: The Guiding of the Holy Spirit
description: The Holy Spirit can feel mysterious, but Jesus promised Him as a Helper who is with you forever. From the pillar of cloud and fire that led Israel through the wilderness to the Spirit who lives inside every believer today, God has always been a guide to His people. This lesson walks through what the Spirit actually does in your life and why you can trust Him to lead you every step of the way.

blocks.text: This is the third lesson in the Trinity arc of Growing Deep. Lesson 1 showed you the Father's love for you, a love that came before you ever knew His name. Lesson 2 showed you the identity Jesus gives, secured by His finished work on the cross. Now this lesson turns to God the Holy Spirit. He is not a vague force or a warm feeling. He is God Himself, the third Person of the Trinity, and He has been given to you for one beautiful purpose: to guide you. From the moment you believed, the Spirit has been walking with you, and He has no intention of stopping.

blocks.alt: Israel gathered in the wilderness at night as the pillar of fire rises before them

blocks.title: The Pillar in the Wilderness
blocks.text:
Israel had just come out of Egypt. Behind them was slavery. Ahead of them was wilderness. They did not have a map, a road sign, or any idea how they were going to make it through the desert. Then God did something beautiful. He did not just give Moses directions and send the people on their own. He went before them. By day there was a pillar of cloud. By night the cloud burned like fire. When the pillar moved, they moved. When it stayed, they stayed.

"And the LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light." (Exodus 13:21)

Picture that scene for a moment. Families, children, animals, tents, fear, and questions, all gathered at the edge of a wilderness they did not understand. They did not need to know the whole route. They only needed to follow the Presence in front of them. Later Scripture says, "At the command of the LORD the people of Israel set out, and at the command of the LORD they camped." (Numbers 9:18) That is the picture at the heart of this lesson. God's people were guided not by certainty, but by His presence.

The pillar that hovered over Israel now lives inside you through the Holy Spirit. He is not a force or a feeling. He is God Himself, and when Jesus returned to the Father, He sent the Spirit so you would never walk alone.
And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper, to be with you forever, even the Spirit of truth.
The Five Actions of the Holy Spirit

Anyone who has hiked an unfamiliar trail knows the feeling. The path narrows, the fog rolls in, and the next marker is nowhere in sight. In those moments you do not need more information. You need a guide, someone who knows the trail, who walks ahead of you, and who will not leave you behind. The Christian life works the same way. You were never meant to walk it alone. The Holy Spirit is your Guide, and the word GUIDE is a simple way to remember the five things He actually does in your life. Walk the trail below, one stop at a time, and let the word build as you go.

G - Glorifies the Savior. The first thing to know about the Holy Spirit is that He always points to Jesus. Jesus Himself said, "He will glorify me, for he will take what is mine and declare it to you" (John 16:14). Any voice that claims to be the Spirit but does not exalt Christ is not Him. When your heart is drawn to love Jesus more, trust Him more, and worship Him more deeply, that is the Holy Spirit at work in you.

U - Unites Sinners to God. You were once far from God, separated by sin and unable to close the gap yourself. The Spirit closed it. Paul writes that you have received "the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, 'Abba! Father!'" (Romans 8:15-16). The Spirit does not just bring you near God. He makes you family. That cry of a child to a father did not come from your flesh. It came from the Spirit of God placed into your heart.

I - Inspires and Illuminates Scripture. The same Spirit who carried along the writers of the Bible now walks with you when you read it. "Men spoke from God as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit" (2 Peter 1:21). When a verse lands on your heart with weight, when an old passage suddenly makes sense, when you see Jesus more clearly on the page than you did the last time you read it, that is the Spirit illuminating Scripture for you.

D - Directs Steps. Paul says, "All who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God" (Romans 8:14). The Spirit does not drag you around or overpower your will. He walks with you, nudges your conscience, gives peace when you are going the right way, and presses a godly discomfort when you are wandering. Remember the pillar that went ahead of Israel in the wilderness? The Spirit does the same thing inside you now. The pillar moved and the people followed. Today, the Spirit moves in your heart the same way, leading you step by step.

E - Eternal Security. The moment you believed in Jesus, something permanent happened. Paul says you were "sealed with the promised Holy Spirit, who is the guarantee of our inheritance" (Ephesians 1:13-14). A seal marks ownership. A guarantee is a down payment that promises the rest is coming. The Spirit in you is God's own pledge that He will finish what He started in you.
3.1 - Which of these five actions of the Spirit do you most need to see right now? Which one has felt most real to you recently, and which one do you want to grow in?
The Spirit at the Jordan and in the Wilderness

And when Jesus was baptized, immediately he went up from the water, and behold, the heavens were opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming to rest on him; and behold, a voice from heaven said, "This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased."

This is the only scene in all of Scripture where the whole Trinity is visible in one single moment. The Son stands in the river, dripping wet from His baptism. The Father speaks from heaven, declaring His love. The Spirit descends like a dove and rests on Jesus. All three Persons, together in one moment. If you have ever wondered whether the Trinity is only a concept, stand at the Jordan in your imagination and watch.
3.2 - What stands out to you about this scene at the Jordan? Where is the Father, where is the Son, and where is the Spirit? What does it tell you about how God is working for you?
Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

The very next sentence in Matthew's Gospel is just as important as what came before. The same Spirit who descended on Jesus at the Jordan immediately led Him into the wilderness. Jesus did not go out under His own steam. The Spirit guided Him. The Son of God walked through a season of hunger and temptation with the Spirit as His Guide. If Jesus Himself needed the Spirit to walk Him through the wilderness, how much more do you and I need the same Spirit in our own seasons of testing?

This is the quiet comfort under all of this teaching. The Spirit who rested on Jesus at the Jordan, the Spirit who led Him into the wilderness and sustained Him there, is the same Spirit now at work in you. When the path fogs over, you are not alone. You have been given a Guide.
3.3 - Where in your life right now does it feel like a wilderness season? What would it look like this week to ask the Holy Spirit to guide you through it the way He guided Jesus?
Lesson 3 Review

God has always been a guide to His people. He led Israel through the wilderness with a pillar they could see. He sent the Spirit to rest on Jesus at the Jordan and to walk Him through the desert. And now that same Spirit lives inside you. He glorifies the Savior so your eyes stay on Jesus. He unites you to the Father so you belong. He inspires and illuminates Scripture so you hear God's voice on the page. He directs your steps so you never walk alone. And He seals your eternal future so nothing can pull you away.

The whole Trinity arc rests on one warm truth. The Father loved you before you ever came. The Son died and rose to bring you in. And the Spirit walks with you now, every step of the way. The pillar that once hovered over a nation now burns inside you. You have been given a Guide, and He will not leave you.
Closing Reflections
What is one key thing you learned from this lesson?
What is one step you are going to take this week?
What is a prayer request for this week?
